event: The Stone Operation (The Cure of Folly)
symbols: stone extraction from head — the medieval quack operation allegedly curing stupidity
funnel-hat on surgeon — the charlatan is himself a fool
nun and monk observing — religious figures complicit in the deception
description: A small circular-framed painting showing a seated man having his head operated on by a surgeon wearing a funnel as a hat. A nun watches holding a book on her head, and a monk or friar observes. A wide landscape stretches behind them. Gothic text inscriptions frame the image above and below. The style is characteristic of Bosch's moral allegories with fine detail and muted colors.

_meta.title: The Stone Operation / The Extraction of the Stone Madness / The Cure of Folly
